name: Gateau De Riz (Rice Pudding)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Short grain white rice, such as Arborio
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak raisins in rum in a bowl for 1 hour.
2. Combine milk, rice, 1/2 cup sugar, and vanilla bean in a medium saucepan.
3. Bring to a boil over medium heat, reduce heat to medium-low, cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until rice absorbs all liquid, about 1 hour.
4. Remove from heat, scrape seeds from vanilla bean into rice, discard pod. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
5. Pour 1 cup sugar into a skillet (shake so the sugar spreads evenly) and place over medium-high heat.
6. Cook, without stirring, until sugar begins to melt, about 2 minutes, then stir with a wooden spoon until golden and just beginning to foam, about 3 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and carefully pour into a 9-inch baking pan, working quickly to cover bottom and sides.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. Stir egg yolks and raisins (discard rum) into rice. Beat egg whites until foamy.
10. Slowly add 1 tablespoon sugar, beat until soft peaks form, then fold into rice mixture.
11. Transfer to caramelized pan. Set pan in a shallow pan of water (bain marie) and bake until a knife inserted into the middle comes out clean, about 1 hour.
12. Cool slightly in pan, then turn out onto a platter. Swirl with Creme Anglaise if desired.
